seoTitle: Sandover vs Santa Teresa — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Sandover and Santa Teresa. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Sandover vs Santa Teresa: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sandover and Santa Teresa.
heading: Sandover vs Santa Teresa — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sandover, Northern Territory and Santa Teresa, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Santa Teresa has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Sandover (30/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sandover or Santa Teresa safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sandover has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Santa Teresa scores 33/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sandover scores 42/100 for bushfire risk. Santa Teresa scores 26/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sandover or Santa Teresa?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Santa Teresa has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
